dom.title: Tuff
dom.text:
Tuff
App Settings

MAIN

Setting
Intelligence
Store
Style
Download Center
VERSION
v2.4.13-beta.1
TUFF

The command bar, reimagined for those who build.

Chromium: 146.0.7680.188
Node.js: 24.15.0
Vue: ^3.5.33
Account

Manage your account information and login status.

登录凭证保护

本地加密保护已启用：凭证由本机 root 密钥加密保存，重启后仍可保持登录状态。

No Account

Account access is currently unavailable.

Login
AI Credits

View personal remaining, used, and quota for Nexus AI credits.

AI Credits

Sign in to view remaining credits and usage.

Sign-in required

Sign in to your Tuff account to read the Nexus credits summary.

Language Settings

Select your preferred language for the application.

Follow System Language

Automatically match the system language setting.

Choose Language

Manually select your preferred display language.

简体中文
English
General Settings

Configure permissions and basic settings

Permissions

Manage file access, system assistive permissions, microphone access, and platform-level privileges in one place.

2/4 granted
Manage Permissions
Auto Start on Boot

Automatically launch app when system starts

Show Tray Icon

Display app icon in system tray

Hide Dock Icon
macOS only

Hide Dock icon after closing main window (macOS only)

Silent Start

Launch application without showing window, run in background only

Auto-mount OmniPanel feature after install

When enabled, the first executable feature from a newly installed plugin is added to OmniPanel automatically.

Hide Noisy System Apps

Hide less-used helper apps like Simulator and system Helper/Agent entries (only hidden from results, index data is kept)

Utilities

Practical tools provide a unified fast desktop arbitrary promoter for you to use.

Custom Placeholder

Customize the CoreBox input placeholder text. Leave empty for default.

Usage

Guide you to use app when enter.

Shortcuts

Manage system-wide shortcut settings in one place.

6 items
Manage
OmniPanel right-click hold duration

Set the hold threshold used to trigger OmniPanel.

Auto paste

Auto-fill fresh clipboard content when CoreBox is opened after copying.

Auto clear

Reset CoreBox on next open after it has been hidden for the selected time. Reopening shortly keeps the current state.

Clipboard polling interval

How often clipboard is checked while idle. Higher frequency uses more power. When CoreBox is visible, polling is boosted to 0.5s.

Clipboard low-battery polling policy

Automatically reduce clipboard polling frequency on battery power to save energy.

Clipboard low-battery interval

Clipboard polling interval used on battery when the low-battery policy is enabled.

Auto hide

Hide searchbar when blur triggered.

Smart Recommendations

Enable smart recommendations based on your usage patterns.

Show Recommendation Reason

Display the reason for each recommendation.

Max Recommendations

Maximum number of recommended items to show in search bar.

Local semantic vectors

Compute a lightweight semantic score from local context features without calling AI services.

AI recommendation rerank

Use a configured AI provider to rerank a small candidate set; failures and timeouts fall back to local ranking.

AI embedding boost

Use embedding capability to improve semantic similarity for a small candidate set. Off by default.

Time context

Use local time slots to rank recommendations without sending data out.

Foreground app context

Prefer actions related to the app you are currently using.

Clipboard context

Use clipboard type and lightweight content hints for local recommendations.

Network context

Adjust recommendations when the device appears offline or on a specific network type.

Bluetooth context

Use local Bluetooth availability as a lightweight device context signal.

Focus context

Prefer work-oriented actions when system focus or do-not-disturb is active.

Power context

Reduce power-heavy recommendations when the device is on battery.

Location bucket

Use a hashed local timezone and network bucket instead of raw location data.

Assistant Floating Ball

Enable a desktop floating entry that sends text commands to CoreBox.

Enable Assistant

Assistant entries such as the floating ball and voice panel only respond when this is enabled. It stays off by default.

Show floating ball

Show a draggable desktop entry. Click it to open the text command panel.

Assistant name

Name shown at the top of the Assistant panel.

Voice wake

When enabled, the floating ball and panel may request speech recognition. When disabled, only text input is used.

Wake words

Separate multiple wake words with commas.

File Index

Manage the file search index

Index Status

Current running state of the file index

Initializing
Index Progress

Scanning (0/1) 0%

Index Statistics

Detailed statistics of file indexing

2:05:48 AM
